text:
> We've spent decades building programming languages that try to do _everything_ — general-purpose, Turing-complete, expressive, fast, safe, concurrent. ...
> But the ambition to make one language handle every problem has a shadow side: it makes us reach for that one language even when a smaller, more targeted tool would serve us better. 
<https://programmingsimplicity.substack.com/p/little-language-case-study-generating?r=1egdky|Little Language Case Study - Generating Code for Simple State Machines>

attachments.fallback: Little Language Case Study - Generating Code for Simple State Machines
attachments.text: Why draw a diagram when you could write 50+ lines of boilerplate instead? 2026-04-08
attachments.title: Little Language Case Study - Generating Code for Simple State Machines

text:
There comes a point in every man’s life where he releases an open source frontend framework. That day, for me, is today.

Tutuca: a zero-dependency, batteries-included SPA framework

<https://marianoguerra.github.io/tutuca/>

Would love your feedback on the framework, the landing page and the tutorial

text: I like the name! (I know what is tutuca since I come from Argentina and it was my favourite snack when I was a kid)
